probleme.titre: Your money is losing value while you hesitate.
probleme.point1Context: the current return on standard savings accounts
probleme.point1Texte: The typical French savings account caps at 1.5% while solid African companies pay out more than 6% in annual dividends. Your money loses purchasing power every month.
probleme.point2Texte: French residents are legally required to declare any foreign securities account. Yet most diaspora investors don't know this — and risk a penalty every year.
probleme.point3Context: clear instructions for investing from the diaspora
probleme.point3Texte: There's no simple guide, no tutorial explaining how to open a brokerage account from Paris or Montreal, and what to buy first.

histoire.p1: As a student in Corporate Finance and Financial Engineering, I study European investment models every day: asset valuation, portfolio management, ETFs, derivatives.
histoire.p2: When I wanted to diversify my portfolio, I looked around. ETFs, French tax-advantaged accounts, MSCI World, European indices… everything pointed to the same Western markets. So I asked a simple question: is there anything connected to Africa?
histoire.p3: The West African stock exchange has existed since 1998. It brings together the leading companies of eight UEMOA countries and pays dividends every year. Yet almost no one talks about it, even within the diaspora.
histoire.p4: With my background, I had the tools to analyze this market. French-language resources built for the diaspora simply didn't exist. I created DiaspoInvest to build them.

faq.sousTitre: Direct answers, no jargon, on how to invest on the BRVM from abroad.

apropos.quote: "Making the African stock market as accessible as investing in Europe."
apropos.contactPrefix: For any question or feedback, write to
paragraphs: I'm Jordan DJIOKAP, a student in Corporate Finance and Financial Engineering. My daily work is analyzing European investment models: asset valuation, portfolio management, ETFs, derivatives.

When I wanted to diversify my own portfolio, I looked for where to invest. ETFs, French tax-advantaged accounts, MSCI World, European indices... everything pointed to the same Western markets. So I asked a simple question: is there anything connected to Africa?

The answer surprised me. The BRVM, the West African regional stock exchange, has existed since 1998. It brings together the leading companies of eight UEMOA countries and pays dividends to its shareholders every year. The average yield is around 6% per year. The BRVM Composite rose 28.89% in 2024 while the CAC 40 posted 0.92%. Yet almost no one talks about it, even within the diaspora.

With my background, I had the tools to analyze this market. What was missing were French-language resources built for someone living outside the UEMOA zone: how to open an account remotely, how to declare income to the tax authorities in France, which stocks to look at first, how to build a long-term regular investment strategy.

I created DiaspoInvest to fill that gap. The Screener lists the 47 listed stocks with their real-time prices and yields. The DCA backtest lets you simulate what a regular monthly investment on any stock since 1998 would have given. The tax calculator estimates the real tax based on the country of residence. The blog covers the topics no one documents in French.

I run this project alone. No conflict of interest with a broker, an SGI or a financial institution. The online tools are free. The guides and the Tracker go further for those who want a complete method.
If you've been looking for a way to invest back home without ever finding a clear answer, you're in the right place.

actionDetail.noteDiv: Dividend of <0>{{div}} FCFA</0> per share (last known fiscal year). For {{cours}} FCFA invested (1 share), the gross yield is <1>{{rendement}}%</1>. In France, this dividend is subject to the flat tax of 31.4% (12.8% income tax + 18.6% social levies since 2026).

calc.titre: How much could your savings earn if invested in the stock market?
calc.intro: This simulator answers 3 different questions. First choose the one you're interested in (step 2 below), then fill in the requested information: the result appears automatically at the bottom.
calc.step1Desc: Search the list below for a company listed on the West African regional stock exchange. Its price (the cost of one share of the company) and its yield fill in automatically.
calc.searchPlaceholder: Search for a stock (name or code)…
calc.coursDesc: This is the price you'd pay today to buy a single share of this company. Filled in automatically, editable if needed.
calc.rendementLabel: Yield: what a share pays out each year
calc.rendementDesc: This is the percentage of the share price that the company pays out to its shareholders each year, as a dividend. The higher this figure, the more the share pays relative to its price.
calc.noDividende: This company has no known dividend — enter a rate yourself to simulate.
calc.step2Title: Step 2 — Choose the question you want answered
calc.step2Desc: Click one of the 3 boxes below. The one you choose becomes the result the simulator will calculate for you; the other two become the information you need to fill in.
calc.trioApport: How much to invest each month
calc.trioHint: The box outlined in gold above is what the simulator will calculate for you.
calc.step3Title: Step 3 — Fill in the requested information
calc.step3Desc: Type an amount directly into the fields below, just like on a regular calculator.
calc.sliderApport: How much do you invest each month?
calc.sliderDivann: What annual dividend income are you aiming for?
calc.resultDesc: Here is the answer calculated from what you chose and filled in above.
calc.labelDivann: Here is the annual income you'd receive in dividends
calc.contextDivann: By investing {{apport}} FCFA every month for {{duree}} years in {{nom}} shares (gross yield of {{taux}}%)
calc.labelApport: Here is the amount to invest each month
calc.contextApport: To earn {{div}} FCFA in dividends every year, after {{duree}} years, with {{nom}} shares
calc.labelDuree: Here is the number of years you'll need
calc.contextDuree: By investing {{apport}} FCFA every month in {{nom}} shares, to reach {{div}} FCFA in dividends per year
calc.kpiActions: Number of shares owned at the end
calc.kpiPortef: Total value of those shares at the end
calc.kpiCapital: Total you will have paid in over time
calc.kpiDivCumul: Total dividends received over the period
calc.ecartExpl: Why is the value of the shares ({{portef}} FCFA) slightly lower than the total paid in ({{capital}} FCFA)? Two reasons: about <0>{{frais}} FCFA</0> went to brokerage fees (0.5% per contribution), and <1>{{reste}} FCFA</1> remain waiting to be invested at the end of the simulation (you can only buy whole shares, never a fraction).
calc.livretIntro: For comparison: here's what the same contributions would have earned in a standard 1.5% savings account over the same period.
calc.disclaimer1: This simulator makes a mathematical projection from the current price and dividend, assuming they stay stable. It is neither a guarantee nor personalized investment advice.
calc.disclaimerSource: Stock prices as of {{date}} · Sources: BRVM.org + sikafinance.com
calc.disclaimerAffil: DiaspoInvest is not affiliated with the BRVM or the CREPMF

blogPreview.sousTitre: Real figures, no jargon. Everything you need to know to invest on the BRVM.

faq.q: What if it's not right for me?
faq.r: Money-back guarantee, no conditions, for 14 days. Email contact@diaspoinvest.fr and you're refunded within 48 hours. No questions asked.

faq.q: I know nothing about the stock market, is this for me?
faq.r: Yes, it's exactly for you. The guide starts from scratch: what the BRVM is, how to open an account from the diaspora, which stocks to look at first. Everything is explained in plain language, no financial jargon.

faq.q: What's the minimum needed to start investing on the BRVM?
faq.r: There's no legal minimum. In practice, with 50,000 to 100,000 FCFA (75 to 150 €) you can buy your first share. Brokerage fees are around 0.5% per order. It's better to start small and regular than to wait until you have a large sum.

faq.q: How do I send money from France to invest?
faq.r: You use an international SWIFT bank transfer to your broker's account, or services like Wave, Orange Money or Wise depending on the broker chosen. The guide details the cheapest and fastest options for the diaspora.

faq.q: Which broker (SGI) do you recommend for the diaspora?
faq.r: I don't recommend any particular broker because this isn't investment advice. The guide compares the criteria to check (digital access, fees, diaspora support) so you can make your own informed choice.

faq.q: Can I invest from France (or abroad)?
faq.r: Yes. Some brokers offer 100% digital access suited to the diaspora. The guide explains the account-opening steps and, above all, how to declare that account in France to stay compliant.

faq.q: Do I have to declare my account to the French tax authorities?
faq.r: Yes, it's mandatory. An account held abroad must be declared via form 3916, or you risk a 1,500 € fine per year. Dividends are taxed under the flat tax (PFU) of 31.4%. The guide details the whole process.

faq.q: Are capital gains taxed in France?
faq.r: Yes, capital gains from selling BRVM shares are taxable in France at 31.4% (flat tax, since 2026). However, they are exempt from tax in the UEMOA zone. The guide explains how to declare them correctly via form 2074.

faq.q: Are the figures up to date?
faq.r: BRVM data is retrieved automatically every trading day from brvm.org. Prices and yields change: always check the latest information with your broker before any decision.
No. DiaspoInvest provides educational content only. Nothing on this site constitutes personalized investment advice. Investing carries a risk of capital loss.
